jobs.title: Senior Product Manager - Banking Foundations
jobs.location: San Francisco, CA, New York, NY, Portland, OR, or Remote within Canada or United States
jobs.description:
Mercury's ability to operate reliable banking* comes down to the infrastructure we build that ensures money moves correctly - every transaction, across every payment rail, with the precision and accountability that customers, regulators, and the financial system itself require. Banking Foundations is the team that builds and owns the platform that makes that possible — the layer that connects Mercury to the Federal Reserve, the payment networks, and our core banking provider, and gives the rest of the company a reliable surface to build on.

We're looking for a Senior Product Manager to own this platform across Mercury. The Banking Foundations platform sits at the intersection of payment rail mechanics, regulatory accountability, and org-wide platform adoption — and the PM who leads our connectivity infrastructure will need to be as comfortable discussing payment rail behavior with engineers as discussing reporting precision with the Treasury team. This is a load-bearing platform role: the decisions made here flow through every payment Mercury makes as a bank* and shape Mercury's future.

*Mercury is a fintech company, not an FDIC-insured bank. Banking services p

jobs.title: Staff Product Manager, Affirm Bank
jobs.description:
Affirm is reinventing credit to make it more honest and friendly, giving consumers the flexibility to buy now and pay later without any hidden fees or compounding interest.

Affirm is building its own bank — and this role sits at the center of that ambition. We're looking for a Staff Product Manager to own the end-to-end bank product: operationalizing a new bank charter within Affirm's ecosystem, launching the deposit product, driving loan originations through the bank entity, and ensuring seamless integration across Affirm's platform. This is a rare opportunity to build a foundational new business line from the ground up — one that will reshape how Affirm funds, serves, and grows relationships with its consumers. You'll work at the intersection of regulated banking, platform engineering, and consumer products to bring Affirm's bank to life.

What You’ll Do

  • Own the end-to-end bank product. Serve as the single-threaded product owner across deposits, loan originations, banking compliance, and operations. You are the person who holds the full picture of how the bank works within Affirm's ecosystem — and you d

jobs.title: Staff Product Manager, Local Processor Acquiring
jobs.description:
Who we are

About Stripe

Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies-from the world's largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups-use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career.

About the Local Processor Acquiring team

Stripe is building a global payments and treasury network-and Local Processor Acquiring (LPA) is how we're rapidly extending that network into the world's fastest-growing markets. The LPA team sits within Global Selling, focused on enabling businesses to accept payments from billions of consumers worldwide without needing to set up local operations.
The problem we're solving is fundamental: the most important payment methods in emerging markets-Korean wallets, PIX in Brazil, UPI in India, QRIS in Indonesia-require local acquiring capabilities that historically took years of work and infrastru

jobs.title: Staff Product Manager, Adaptive Pricing
jobs.description:
Who we are

About Stripe

Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies-from the world's largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups-use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career.

About the Adaptive Pricing team

Stripe is building a global payments and treasury network, and the Adaptive Pricing team sits at the heart of Global Selling-focused on helping businesses optimize how they sell across borders.
One of the hardest problems in global commerce is pricing. Businesses must navigate different currencies, foreign exchange dynamics, setting proper price points, aligning prices with local standards, and unlocking local payment methods-all while maintaining a seamless buyer experience. Getting this wrong means lost revenue, abandoned carts, and failed payments.

jobs.title: Staff Product Manager, Payments
jobs.description:
Who we are

About Stripe

Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies - from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups - use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career.

About the Organization

The Payments organization focuses on developing products and platforms that enable users to accept payments from customers efficiently. This includes building APIs for processing payments, enabling regional, non-card payment options, and extending Stripe's capabilities to make it easy for businesses to accept in-person payments. Optimized Checkout and Link teams work to create best-in-class checkout experiences that enhance customer satisfaction and drive merchant conversion rates.

jobs.title: Staff Product Manager, Banking
jobs.location: Menlo Park, CA; New York, NY; Washington, DC
jobs.description:
Join us in building the future of finance.

Our mission is to democratize finance for all. An estimated $124 trillion of assets will be inherited by younger generations in the next two decades. The largest transfer of wealth in human history. If you’re ready to be at the epicenter of this historic cultural and financial shift, keep reading.

About the team + role

Robinhood's Money team is building the next generation of banking and spending products for all Americans. Our team members drive never-before-seen products and features from concept to launch to hyperscale. As a Product Manager, you will play a central role in shaping the future of our core banking product and the broader platform that powers it. You'll help define the systems, capabilities, and product foundations that enable new banking experiences, while partnering cross-functionally to bring products from concept through launch and scale.

jobs.title: Senior Product Manager - API & Agentic Banking
jobs.location: San Francisco, CA, New York, NY, Portland, OR, or Remote within Canada or United States
jobs.description:
At Mercury, we are building the banking* stack for startups and doing so with a focus on building a high-craft, simple, and powerful product experience.

We believe the future of financial software is infrastructure that works on your behalf. Mercury's API is how our most ambitious customers interact with us programmatically: pulling transaction data, managing accounts, and increasingly, connecting Mercury to AI-powered workflows through integrations like MCP. We're building toward a Mercury that customers and AI agents can talk to, ask questions of, and take action through.

We're looking for a product manager to own Mercury's API, our integrations with the broader AI and developer tool ecosystem, and the AI-powered product experiences we're building to make Mercury programmable and intelligent. This is a role for someone who's been building in the API and AI space already and who sees the opportunity to make a bank programmable as one of the most interesting product problems available right now.

*Mercury is a fintech company, not an FDIC-insured bank. Banking services provided through Choice Financial Group and Column N.A., Members FDIC.

jobs.title: Head of Product, Developer Experience
jobs.description:
About Adyen

Adyen is the financial technology platform of choice for leading companies. By providing end-to-end payments capabilities, data-driven insights, and financial products in a single global solution, Adyen helps businesses achieve their ambitions faster. With offices around the world, Adyen serves customers including Facebook, Uber, Spotify, and Microsoft.

The Role

Adyen is seeking a visionary product leader to serve as our Head of Product for Developer Experience. Our developer platform is the foundation upon which thousands of businesses build their payment and financial systems. As our customers increasingly use AI-powered development tools to build their integrations, we are looking for an AI-native leader to set the strategy and accelerate the evolution of our platform.

You will be responsible for the entire developer journey, from onboarding to the first API call to scaling globally.
